title: Non-English OA cancer article inclusion (multilingual)
context: The current corpus pipeline is English-first. A significant portion of PMC OA and Europe PMC OA cancer literature is published in other languages (especially Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese, French, German). Including non-English articles requires language-aware MeSH mapping, a language-aware patient-content detector, and a reviewer with appropriate language competence. This is a backlog item requiring additional tooling and reviewer capacity before it can proceed.
objective: Extend the corpus pipeline to include non-English OA cancer articles: add language detection, language-aware MeSH mapping, language-aware patient-content detection, and a multilingual datasheet section — with a qualified reviewer covering the target languages.
acceptanceCriteria: Language detection is added to the ingestion pipeline; detected language is recorded on every canonical record.

MeSH mapping is verified to work correctly for non-English articles (MeSH is language-neutral, but journal/title metadata may be in the source language); any language-specific edge cases are documented.
The patient-content detector's signal set is reviewed and extended for non-English article structures (JATS is mostly language-neutral, but heuristics may need tuning).
A qualified reviewer covering the target language(s) is named and their sign-off on the language-specific handling is recorded before the multilingual corpus is released.
The datasheet is updated to document the language composition of the corpus and any language-specific limitations.
All existing gate-002 and patient-008 tests continue to pass; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; DCO signed-off.
resources: pmc-cancer-query-003 (MeSH C04 query — language-neutral at MeSH level)
pmc-cancer-patient-008 (patient-content detector to extend for multilingual)
PLAN.md (Open questions — non-English OA cancer articles)
Europe PMC multilingual OA holdings documentation
output: An updated corpus release including non-English OA cancer articles from PMC OA / Europe PMC OA, with language detection, language-aware metadata handling, extended patient-content detection, and an updated multilingual datasheet section.
